description: executive order issued by president Abraham Lincoln on January 1, 1863 that freed southern slaves
45th Governor of Alabama (1919–1998)
city in and county seat of Jefferson County, Alabama, United States
description_wiki: Birmingham ( BUR-ming-ham) is a city in the north central region of Alabama, United States.

description: northwest section of the U.S. state of Mississippi
description_wiki: The Mississippi Delta, also known as the Yazoo–Mississippi Delta, or simply the Delta, is the distinctive northwest section of the U.S. state of Mississippi (and portions of Arkansas and Louisiana) that lies between the Mississippi and Yazoo rivers.

description: Birmingham, Alabama public safety commissioner during the Civil Rights Movement (1897-1973)
description_wiki: Theophilus Eugene "Bull" Connor (July 11, 1897 – March 10, 1973) was an American politician who was Commissioner of Public Safety for the city of Birmingham, Alabama, for more than two decades.

description: American white supremacist hate group
description_wiki: The Ku Klux Klan (KKK; ), sometimes referred to as the Klan, is an American Protestant-led white supremacist and far-right hate group.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: bicameral legislature of the United States
description_wiki: The United States Congress is the legislative branch of the federal government of the United States.

description: maritime land force service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Marine Corps (USMC), also referred to as the United States Marines or simply the Marines, is the naval infantry service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

aliases: Ground forces of the United States Armed Forces
description: land service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Army (U.S. Army) is the land service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: Civil rights activist (1922-2011)
description_wiki: Freddie Lee Shuttlesworth (born Freddie Lee Robinson, March 18, 1922 – October 5, 2011) was an American Baptist minister and civil rights activist who led fights against segregation and other forms of racism, during the civil rights movement.

description: American civil rights activist and minister (1926–1990)
American Baptist minister and civil rights leader (1929–1968)
civil rights movement in Nashville, Tennessee
description_wiki: The Nashville Student Movement was an organization that challenged legalized racial segregation in Nashville, Tennessee, during the Civil Rights Movement.

description: U.S. activists who rode interstate buses in interracial groups to show the continuing prevalence of segregation
city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: city in and county seat of Cuyahoga County, Ohio, United States
description_wiki: Cleveland ( KLEEV-lənd) is a city in the U.S. state of Ohio and the county seat of Cuyahoga County.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: city in Ohio, United States; county seat of Hamilton County
description_wiki: Cincinnati (  SIN-sih-NAT-ee; colloquially nicknamed Cincy) is the most populous city in Hamilton County, Ohio, United States, and its county seat.

description: NBC White Paper is a documentary TV Series on NBC.
description_wiki: NBC White Paper is a documentary television series on the NBC television network lasting from 1960 to 1989.
